data.title: Top gaining Russell 2000 stocks as index close to strongest performance vs. large-caps
data.description:
The Russell 2000 (IMW) is close to its strongest relative performance compared to the S&P 500 (SP500) since last November.

Small-caps are already hitting their highest levels since March relative to their large-cap counterparts (IWB).

 

 

The Russell 2000 (NYSEARCA:IWM) has been mostly flat on Thursday and up 6.8% year-to-date.

Here are the best performing stocks within the index:

data.title: Nike to cut corporate workforce by 1% - report
data.description:
As part of CEO Elliott Hill’s efforts to realign the business and “create the next great chapter” for the iconic sportswear company, Nike (NYSE:NKE) is reportedly planning to let go of 1% of its corporate staff, according to CNBC.

The latest job cuts follow Nike's (NYSE:NKE) initiative to realign the business into dedicated cross-functional teams by sport as part of its “Win Now” turnaround strategy.

The new structure shifts Nike’s focus from demographic categories, like Men’s, Women’s and Kids, into five fields of play – running, basketball, football, training, and sportswear -- with the objective of developing products for specific athletes and customers. As such, the company’s corporate structure will change from Consumer and Sport, Marketing, Product Creation, into three core brands, Nike, Jordan, and Converse.

By changing the alignment, Hill hopes to reinvigorate the innovation pipeline, which was stagnant under the prior leadership and blamed for the company’s anemic growth.

As part of these changes, some positions will be eliminated while some staff will be moved into new positions. Nike (NYSE:NKE) staff will learn if their position has been impacted by September 8 with the majority of new roles taking effect September 21. Accordingly, to “make space for these conversations,” corporate employees based in an office in the U.S, and Canada will work remotely during the week.

The news had little impact on Nike (NKE) shares which are holding onto a modest gain.

data.title: Dollar General signals EPS guidance increase to $5.80–$6.30, expands delivery reach with Uber Eats partnership
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dollar General Corporation (DG) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Todd J. Vasos highlighted strong second quarter results, stating net sales increased 5.1% to $10.7 billion and same-store sales rose 2.8%, driven by growth in both customer traffic and average basket. Vasos noted, "We grew market share in both dollars and units in highly consumable product sales once again during the quarter, in addition to growing market share in nonconsumable product sales."
  • Vasos emphasized balanced category growth, positive comps in all major categories, and new store performance, adding, "We were excited to see a second consecutive quarter of broad-based category growth with positive comp sales in each of our consumables, seasonal, home and apparel categories."
  • The company continues to focus on value, maintaining over 2,000 SKUs at or below the $1 price point, with Value Valley merchandising seeing same-store sales growth more than twice the company rate.
  • Vasos announced a CFO transition, welcoming Donny Lau as the next CFO beginning in October while thanking current CFO Kelly Dilts for her leadership.
  • CFO Kelly M. Dilts stated, "For Q2, gross profit as a percentage of sales was 31.3%, an increase of 137 basis points. This increase was primarily attributable to lower shrink, higher inventory markups and lower inventory damages."
  • Dilts noted operating profit for the quarter increased 8.3% to $595 million, and EPS increased 9.4% to $1.86.

Outlook

  • The company raised its full-year 2025 guidance, now expecting net sales growth of 4.3% to 4.8%, same-store sales growth of 2.1% to 2.6%, and EPS in the range of $5.80 to $6.30.
  • Dilts indicated, "While we're not providing specific quarterly guidance, the low end of our sales and earnings guidance ranges allow for increasing pressure on consumer spending as we move through the back half of the year with Q4 potentially more impacted than Q3."
  • Capital spending is expected to remain in the $1.3 billion to $1.4 billion range, with approximately 4,885 real estate projects planned, including 575 new U.S. store openings and up to 15 in Mexico.

Financial Results

  • Gross profit as a percentage of sales rose to 31.3%, an increase of 137 basis points year-over-year, primarily due to lower shrink and higher inventory markups.
  • Operating profit increased to $595 million, with EPS at $1.86.
  • Cash flow from operations for the first half reached $1.8 billion, up 9.8% from the prior year, and inventories decreased to $6.6 billion.
  • The company returned approximately $130 million to shareholders through dividends.

Q&A

  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Asked about shrink contribution to operating margin and long-term margin targets. Dilts replied, "We are definitely optimistic that we could potentially outperform on shrink and get a little bit more than those 80 basis points over the mid- to longer term. But we're still targeting that long-term framework 6% to 7% on the operating margin."
  • Simeon Gutman, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about gross margin seasonality and execution progress. Dilts explained, "We're certainly expecting a year-over-year improvement in both of the quarters. But... we actually have tougher laps in Q4 on the gross margin front."
  • Rupesh Parikh, Oppenheimer: Focused on digital delivery partnerships. Vasos emphasized, "DoorDash has been really the start of our digital journey... We saw a 60% year-over-year increase on that platform... We just signed a deal with Uber Eats... 4,000 stores up and running. And by the end of the third quarter, we'll have 14,000 stores..."
  • Matthew Boss, JPMorgan: Asked about customer cohorts and value proposition. Vasos said, "Our trade-in has been accelerating over the last few quarters... We're as good as ever across all classes of trade on our everyday price and our customers resonate with that very nicely."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were probing on sustainability of gross margin improvement, shrink benefits, and digital strategy, with a neutral to slightly positive tone given the strong performance and raised guidance.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, focusing on execution, margin improvement, and digital initiatives, with statements like "We are definitely optimistic" and "I feel really good about that as well."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was more confident, especially regarding shrink mitigation and digital growth, while analysts shifted from cautious to more constructive questioning.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for net sales, same-store sales, and EPS was raised this quarter, compared to a more cautious tone in Q1 due to tariff uncertainties.
  • Gross profit margin improvement was more pronounced in Q2, with shrink reduction exceeding prior expectations.
  • Digital and delivery initiatives expanded, with the addition of Uber Eats and accelerated rollout of DG delivery services.
  • Management's confidence and focus on sustainability of recent gains was more pronounced, while analysts' questions evolved from defensive (tariffs, margin pressure) to strategic (digital, long-term margin targets).

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted potential uncertainty in consumer spending, especially in Q4.
  • Incentive compensation is expected to be a $200 million headwind in 2025.
  • General liability claims are trending more expensive, though not materially impacting results, and all such trends are factored into guidance.
  • Tariff environment remains dynamic, but management is mitigating cost impacts through sourcing and vendor negotiations.

Final Takeaway

Dollar General reported strong second quarter performance with broad-based growth, significant improvement in gross margin through shrink reduction, and clear gains in digital delivery channels. The company raised its full-year guidance for net sales and EPS, reflecting higher confidence in ongoing initiatives and operating leverage, while also announcing a CFO transition. Management remains focused on optimally balancing value for customers, investing in growth, and sustaining margin improvements amid evolving economic and industry dynamics.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: KPop Demon Hunters is Netflix's most popular streaming film ever and a force at the box office
data.description:
KPop Demon Hunters has become the most-watched movie of all time on the Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) streaming platform after tallying an extraordinary 236 million views in just 67 days since its June 20 release. That tally blew past the previous record-holder Red Notice's 230.9 million views. Interestingly, the viewership momentum was largely organic as the film started on a slow trajectory following its June 20 debut.

The animated musical, about a K-pop girl group who fight demons, has achieved rapid and unprecedented global chart success for both its film and soundtrack. When Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) released a sing-along version theatrically, it earned over $19 million in just one weekend across about 1,700 theaters to top the box office charts. 

KPop Demon Hunters was primarily produced by Sony Pictures Animation (SONY) and released by Netflix (NFLX). The film was produced as part of a $100 million deal struck between Sony (SONY) and Netflix (NFLX) in 2021 for direct-to-streaming films. Talks of a KPop sequel are reportedly already taking place.

The theatrical release provided a late summer boost for Marcus Theatres (MCS), Regal Cinemas, Alamo Drafthoues, and some Cinemark (CNK) theaters, while AMC Entertainment (AMC) missed out on the buzz by not sliding KPop Demon Hunters into its schedule.

data.title: Ørsted upgraded to Buy at Berenberg after stock's recent plunge
data.description:
Ørsted (OTCPK:DNNGY) +5.6% in Copenhagen trading Thursday as Berenberg upgraded shares to Buy from Hold with a DKK225 price target, after the shares plunged 16% following the Trump administration's order to halt all activities related to its Revolution Wind project off the coast of Rhode Island.

While acknowledging the risks tied to the U.S. portfolio, Berenberg said Ørsted's (OTCPK:DNNGY) DKK60B rights issue announced on August 11 strengthens the company's balance sheet and provides funding capacity for capital expenditures through 2027.

The brokerage said the capital raise will add ~DKK60B to Ørsted's (OTCPK:DNNGY) finances, allowing it to cover investment needs until 2028 even in a worst-case scenario that would included cancellations of both the Sunrise Wind and Revolution Wind projects in the U.S.

Ørsted's (OTCPK:DNNGY) funding strategy relies on partial sales of its Hornsea 3 project in the U.K. and Greater Changhua 2 project in Taiwan, along with the disposal of its European onshore portfolio, but confirmation of the farm-downs and disposals likely will provide positive catalysts over the next 6-12 months, Berenberg said.

data.title: DICK'S Sporting Goods raises 2025 comp sales outlook to 2%-3.5% as Foot Locker acquisition nears close
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: DICK'S Sporting Goods (DKS) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Edward W. Stack reported, "As announced earlier this morning, we delivered a very strong second quarter with comps of 5%. Our momentum continues to build, which is a clear reflection of the strength of our long-term strategies and investments." Stack provided an update on the pending acquisition of Foot Locker, stating, "Foot Locker shareholders approved the transaction, we've also received all regulatory approvals, and we anticipate the transaction will close on September 8. We remain very enthusiastic about the strategic benefits from the deal."

  • Lauren R. Hobart stated, "We are very pleased with our strong Q2 results... Today, we're raising our full year outlook. This updated guidance reflects our strong Q2 results and the ongoing confidence we have in our business, grounded in our team's execution of the four strategic pillars I just mentioned. We now expect comp sales growth for the year to be in the range of 2% to 3.5% and EPS to be in the range of $13.90 to $14.50."

  • Navdeep Gupta said, "We are very pleased to deliver a consolidated sales increase of 5% to $3.65 billion... Gross profit for the second quarter remained strong at $1.35 billion or 37.06% of net sales and increased 33 basis points from last year... On a non-GAAP basis, SG&A expenses increased 9.9% to $864 million and deleveraged 105 basis points compared to last year's non-GAAP results."

Outlook

  • The company raised its full-year comp sales growth guidance to a range of 2% to 3.5%, up from the prior expectation of 1% to 3%. Full-year EPS guidance was also raised to a range of $13.90 to $14.50, compared to the previous range of $13.80 to $14.40. Consolidated sales are now expected to be between $13.75 billion and $13.95 billion, previously $13.6 billion to $13.9 billion. Management stated, "Our updated guidance reflects our strong Q2 performance and includes the expected impact from all tariffs currently in effect."

Financial Results

  • The company reported consolidated sales of $3.65 billion for Q2, a 5% increase. Comparable sales grew 5%, driven by a 4.1% increase in average ticket and a 0.9% increase in transactions. Gross profit was $1.35 billion, or 37.06% of net sales, expanding 33 basis points. Non-GAAP EPS was $4.38, essentially flat from $4.37 last year. Inventory increased 7.1% year-over-year. The company reported ending Q2 with $1.2 billion in cash and cash equivalents and no borrowings on its $2 billion credit facility. Net capital expenditures were $213 million, and $96 million was paid in dividends.

Q&A

  • Brian William Nagel, Oppenheimer: Asked about Foot Locker integration and revitalization plans. Lauren R. Hobart responded, "We see a tremendous opportunity with the Foot Locker business... We plan to invest in stores. We plan to invest in marketing, and we know that there are opportunities from a core merchandising standpoint."

  • Nagel also inquired about tariffs and mitigation efforts. Hobart noted, "We've seen some sporadic price increases, but they are surgical and not across the board, and we're seeing our consumer, obviously, with a 5% comp, we're seeing the consumer respond really well."

  • Simeon Ari Gutman, Morgan Stanley: Asked about second-half comp assumptions and consumer trends. Hobart replied, "We are not seeing any signs of slowdown with the consumer... We saw growth across all of our key segments."

  • Gutman also questioned gross margin expansion and expense leverage. Navdeep Gupta explained, "Our margins expanded 33 basis points, gross margins. And the merch margin expanded by 18 basis points."

  • Adrienne Eugenia Yih-Tennant, Barclays: Asked about industry trends, brand relationships, and price increases. Hobart said, "Our vertical brands also still have 700 to 900 basis points higher margin than the average national brand... As the consumer continues to vote with -- into the categories that we serve... we feel terrific."

  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Asked for clarification on gross margin outlook. Gupta stated, "We still expect the gross margin to expand on a full year basis... we are balancing several puts and takes as we navigate the landscape that we have in front of us between tariffs, our consistent focus on keeping our inventory really vibrant..."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts frequently expressed positive sentiment, with multiple congratulations on results and constructive questions about integration, consumer trends, and margin outlook. Concerns focused on Foot Locker integration and gross margin guidance language.

  • Management tone was confident in prepared remarks, emphasizing momentum and strategic execution. During Q&A, management remained upbeat and addressed concerns directly, with Hobart stating, "We are very pleased with our strong Q2 results," and Gupta providing detailed explanations on margin and expense trends. The tone remained consistent with the previous quarter’s emphasis on strategic execution and confidence, with current quarter remarks slightly more optimistic due to raised guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for comp sales and EPS was raised versus the previous quarter’s reaffirmation. The Q2 call reflected increased confidence, citing strong Q2 results and broad-based growth. Gross margin expectation language shifted from an explicit 75 basis points improvement to a more general expectation of expansion. Strategic focus areas remained consistent: omnichannel growth, store portfolio repositioning, category growth, and e-commerce acceleration. Analysts in both quarters focused on Foot Locker integration, tariffs, and margin sustainability. Management’s tone in Q2 was more positive, buoyed by increased guidance and strong performance across key categories.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariffs and their impact on pricing and demand were discussed, with management noting, "We've seen some sporadic price increases, but they are surgical and not across the board."

  • Integration of Foot Locker and the timing and realization of synergies were raised, with management reiterating a cautious approach until after the deal closes September 8.

  • Gross margin guidance language change was highlighted by analysts, and management emphasized balancing puts and takes amid dynamic macroeconomic conditions.

Final Takeaway

DICK'S Sporting Goods delivered strong sales and margin performance in Q2 2025, raising its full-year outlook for both comp sales and EPS. The company emphasized broad-based growth across all key categories, continued e-commerce acceleration, and strong performance from vertical brands. Management highlighted enthusiasm around the pending Foot Locker acquisition, with strategic plans to drive store and merchandising improvements post-close. Risks from tariffs and integration remain, but management expressed confidence in both its current business momentum and long-term growth strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Trump's Fed nominee on track for Senate confirmation before September meeting - report
data.description:
The Republican-controlled U.S. Senate is poised to confirm Stephen Miran, President Donald Trump's nominee to replace former Federal Reserve Governor Adriana Kugler, in time for the Federal Open Market Committee's Sept. 16-17 meeting, according to a media report on Thursday.

Democrats have privately acknowledged that they won't be able to block the confirmation, though they may be able to delay it by a few days, Bloomberg reported. The Senate Banking Committee is expected to take the matter up sometime next week.

Miran received Senate confirmation earlier this year for his role of chair of the Council of Economic Advisers. No Republicans voted against him.

In the confirmation hearings, he's likely to be pressed on his views of Federal Reserve independence and the White House's efforts to pressure the central bank to lower interest rates.

Some Republicans, who have previously defended the Fed's independence, may also question Miran about that issue.

While the GOP holds a 53-47 majority in the Senate, Trump's nominees can only afford to lose three votes. Most of the president's nominees, even controversial ones, have been confirmed in the Senate in the president's second term.

Recently, trading activity has implied an 85.2% probability of a 25-basis-point rate cut in September, up from a 75.0% chance a week ago, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion

data.title: Sen. Graham urges tariffs on Norway after wealth fund drops Caterpillar
data.description:
U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham, a close ally of President Donald Trump, has called for new trade penalties against Norway following its sovereign wealth fund’s decision to divest from Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT).

In a social media post Thursday, the South Carolina Republican suggested the U.S. impose further tariffs on Norwegian goods and even restrict visas for executives tied to the $2 trillion wealth fund and “other organizations that attempt to punish American companies for geopolitical differences.”

Norway’s fund announced this week it was excluding Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT) from its portfolio, citing the use of the company’s bulldozers in demolitions of Palestinian property in Gaza and the West Bank. As of June 30, the fund’s stake in the Texas-based machinery giant was valued at about $2.1 billion.

Washington already has a 15% tariff in place on imports from Norway while trade talks continue.

Trump has a history of reacting when congressional allies pressure U.S. companies or foreign governments. For example, he publicly called for Intel (INTC) Chief Executive Lip-Bu Tan to resign after Senator Tom Cotton raised concerns about Tan’s China-linked investments. Trump later praised Tan after Intel (INTC) agreed to provide the U.S. with an ownership stake.

Norway’s sovereign wealth fund, managed by Norges Bank Investment Management, follows ethical guidelines set by parliament, with recommendations from an independent ethics council. Its exclusions range from companies tied to land mines to those involved in climate risks.

As of June 30, U.S. equities and bonds made up more than half of the fund’s portfolio, Bloomberg News reported.

data.title: 'Golden share' makes ZIM a tough takeover target, CEO Glickman says
data.description:
The Israeli government's "golden share" in ZIM Integrated Shipping (NYSE:ZIM) requires that the majority of the board are Israeli, including the Chairman and CEO, which Eli Glickman said narrows down the field of people who may be interested in the company, TradeWinds reported Thursday.

ZIM (NYSE:ZIM) President and CEO Eli Glickman declined to comment on a rumored privatization effort linking him with Israeli shipping magnate Abraham Unger, but "this is a high-risk market," he said, according to the report. "Not that many people are going to take this risk."

Glickman also noted that it took Idan Ofer, whose Kenon Holding was ZIM's (ZIM) largest shareholder until selling its 20.7% stake late last year, 20 years to recover his investment in the company after his interests took control of the company in 2004.

The restrictive provisions involved in any transfer of ZIM's (ZIM) ownership may have taken its toll in the financial markets, with shares slumping 22% since buyout rumors surfaced on August 11.

Ungar reportedly sought to buy ZIM (ZIM), take it private, then merge it with his company Ray Shipping in a cash-and-stock deal, potentially bringing in Greek investors to accomplish the transaction.

data.title: Build-A-Bear increases 2025 guidance, targets at least 60 new locations amid record Q2 results
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc. (BBW) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Sharon Price John, President, CEO & Director, opened by highlighting, "we are pleased to report the best second quarter and first half results in Build-A-Bear's history, achieving record revenue while simultaneously expanding pretax margin and earnings per share." John attributed the performance to the company's focus on its three strategic pillars: a capital-light partner-operated retail model for global expansion, expanded digital transformation and social media-driven omnichannel sales, and new product initiatives like the Mini Beans collection.
  • John announced, "we are increasing our 2025 guidance at this juncture," citing strong business momentum and an improved outlook for net new unit growth.
  • J. Christopher Hurt, Chief Operations & Experience Officer, detailed that "in the quarter, 14 net new experience locations were opened, of which 86% were international," and noted expansion into 32 countries, with plans for a multilevel workshop at ICON Park in Orlando in 2026.
  • Hurt stated, "given that our 2025 expansion plans are exceeding expectations, we are increasing our net new unit growth guidance for the year to at least 60 locations from the previous 50."
  • Voin Todorovic, Chief Financial Officer, emphasized, "this was the most profitable second quarter and first half in the company's history," and reported, "during the quarter, we returned $6 million through dividends and share repurchases, bringing our year-to-date total to $13.1 million."

Outlook

  • Management increased revenue and pretax income guidance, now expecting revenue growth in the "range of mid-single to high single digits."
  • Pretax income guidance was raised to "$62 million to $70 million, which assumes the current tariff rates to be in effect for the rest of our fiscal year" (CFO Todorovic).
  • The company lifted its target for net new unit growth in 2025 to "at least 60 locations from the previous 50" (COO Hurt).
  • Management continues to expect the majority of new unit growth to be partner-operated, primarily internationally.

Financial Results

  • Total revenues for Q2 were $124.2 million, an increase of 11.1% year-over-year (CFO Todorovic).
  • Net retail sales were $114.6 million, up 10.8%, with commercial revenue growing 18.3%.
  • Gross margin was 57.6%, up 340 basis points, driven by improved merchandise margin, reduced promotional activity, and leverage of fixed costs.
  • Pretax income grew 32.7% to $15.3 million, and EPS was $0.94, an increase of 46.9%.
  • Cash and cash equivalents at quarter end totaled $39.1 million, an increase of 55.4% compared to the same period last year.
  • Inventory was $81.8 million, with about half the dollar increase attributable to tariffs.
  • The company finished the quarter with no borrowings under its revolving credit facility.

Q&A

  • Eric Martin Beder, Small Cap Consumer Research, LLC: Asked about consumer response to price increases and maturity of partner-operated stores. John explained selective and strategic price increases, focusing on value and managing entry-level price points, while Todorovic noted, "we really haven't seen negative impact of some of the selective price changes."
  • Beder inquired about the maturity and impact of partner-operated stores. Hurt responded, "as these stores start to mature, we've seen very great response from our partners as they are adding more locations."
  • Gregory Thomas Gibas, Northland Capital Markets: Asked about Mini Beans sales and wholesale opportunities. John said, "we've seen an 80% increase of Mini Beans on a year-on-year basis," and Hurt noted successful expansion of Mini Beans into international and wholesale channels, including over 50 Applegreen stores.
  • Gibas also queried e-commerce momentum. Todorovic reported a 15.1% increase in e-commerce demand and ongoing efforts to advance digital transformation and omnichannel strategy.
  • Keegan Tierney Cox, D.A. Davidson: Questioned implied guidance for the second half and partner-operated store momentum. Todorovic cited tougher comps and macro uncertainties, especially tariffs, as reasons for expected margin pressure. He pointed to a nearly $16 million headwind from tariffs and labor costs, but expressed confidence in meeting or exceeding last year's full-year profitability at the high end of guidance.
  • Steven Silver, Argus Research Company: Asked about potential for company-operated store expansion outside the U.S. Todorovic stated, "we are definitely open in considering operating stores," but current focus remains on partner-operated expansion due to ROI and local market expertise.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on tariffs, pricing, partner-operated stores, and product performance, with a slightly positive to neutral tone. Questions often highlighted challenges but acknowledged strong results and guidance increases.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing record performance and strategic discipline. During Q&A, management addressed concerns directly, with Todorovic stating, "we are doing things that's within our control," and John reiterating, "we remain confident in our continued efforts."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts maintain a confident and constructive tone, with slightly more focus on cost pressures and tariffs this quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from reiteration to an increase for both revenue and pretax income, with a higher net new unit target (60 vs. 50).
  • Strategic focus remains on global expansion, digital transformation, and leveraging brand equity, but more detail was provided on successful product launches and international partner growth.
  • Analysts this quarter pressed further on margin pressures, tariff impacts, and partner store performance, compared to a broader focus last quarter on retail channel and Mini Beans expansion.
  • Key metrics such as revenue, EPS, and gross margin improved sequentially. Management's confidence level remained high, with increased clarity on mitigation strategies for external headwinds.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited tariffs as a significant headwind, with the impact expected to be less than $11 million for fiscal 2025, plus $5 million in additional labor and medical costs, totaling nearly $16 million in headwinds.
  • Tariff costs are mitigated through dual sourcing, inventory pull-forward, and leveraging international expansion.
  • Macroeconomic uncertainties and inflationary pressures remain ongoing risks, but management expressed comfort with inventory levels and flexibility in capital allocation.
  • Analysts signaled ongoing concern about margin pressure, guidance for the second half, and the sustainability of international and product-driven growth.

Final Takeaway

Build-A-Bear Workshop delivered its strongest second quarter ever, increasing revenue and pretax income guidance for 2025 while signaling at least 60 net new locations, predominantly internationally and through partner-operated models. The company credits its capital-light expansion, successful product launches like Mini Beans, and robust omnichannel strategy for driving record results despite tariff and cost headwinds. With continued investment in brand initiatives and digital transformation, management remains focused on profitable growth and shareholder returns for the remainder of the year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
